Implementation Details: Interactive UI for symbol/timeframe selection; renders custom Lightweight Charts overlay; displays live AI explanation; signal reasons; warnings; metric strip; and tabs.
Formats signal bundle fields into presenter-friendly rows for display in the sidebar or metrics strip.
Orchestrates the data fetch; calls the SMC; indicators; session; and risk engines; validates the signal; and caches responses.
Computes final BUY/SELL/HOLD signals; aggregates confidence scores; and sets initial trade levels based on strategy confirmations.
Identifies swing points (HH, HL, LH, LL); detects Break of Structure (BOS); and draws structure trend lines.
Identifies major support and resistance zones based on historical touch points and candle bodies.
Tracks active trading sessions (Sydney, Tokyo, London, New York) and identifies session high/low breakouts.
Applies tailored strategies (e.g., London breakout, NY trend ride) depending on active sessions.
Calculates indicators locally (EMA 21/50/200, RSI 14, MACD, ATR 14, Bollinger Bands, Volume Averages).
Calculates and maps indicator states (bullish/bearish/neutral) to support decision logic.
Determines position size based on account balance and risk %; checks risk-reward ratios; and flags distance limit breaches.
Calculates ATR-based stop loss and take profit targets; validates trade feasibility; and ensures R:R >= 1:2.
Handles live candle feeds; aggregates timeframes; and applies local CSV file fallback data if APIs fail.
Fetches and parses macroeconomic events from Forex Factory to highlight news risk zones.
Evaluates news release schedules to block or flag trades during high-impact news windows.
Bootstraps database repositories; market data feeds; and orchestrator services into a central container.
Connects directly to local MetaTrader 5 terminal to retrieve tick data and candles.
Sends technical analysis data to Gemini/LLM to generate a detailed trade rationale explanation.
Runs LLM prompt validation on technical outputs to double-check structural trade bias.
Templates defining the exact prompt structure, JSON format, and constraints for the AI model.
Saves and loads active and historic trading signals to JSONL database records.
Saves and retrieves historical economic calendar releases.
Logs simulated or executed trades, entry/exit timestamps, and P&L results.
Simulates strategy rules against historical candle data; returns win rate, profit factor, drawdown, and average R:R.
Centralizes definitions for SYMBOLS, TIMEFRAMES, account balances, risk parameters, and environment paths.
Stores private API keys, local MT5 credentials, and platform flags.
